original_image_tags: A standard typewritten government memorandum on aged paper. There are several handwritten annotations and official stamps.

The bottom right of the page contains several stamped and handwritten notations. A purple stamp reads "RECORDED - 103". Next to it, a red stamp with a date reads "AUG 4 1950". Handwritten in red ink next to this is "162-83 894 - 241". Below these is a handwritten note in black ink, "EX-71". At the bottom left, a large red stamp reads "63 AUG 10 1950".

elements.description: A standard US government office memorandum form (Standard Form No. 64). The page contains typed text, a red stamp reading 'RECORDED - 103', a red handwritten date stamp 'AUG 4 1950', a pink date stamp '63 AUG 10 1950', and various handwritten annotations. There are no photographs, sketches, diagrams, or other graphical visual elements.

judge.reason: Both agree on the presence and text of three stamps (purple 'RECORDED - 103', red 'AUG 4 1950', and red '63 AUG 10 1950') and handwritten annotations, but B omits the specific handwritten text '162-83 894 - 241' and 'EX-71' described by A, which is an omission, not a contradiction.
